front: Play: about the Younger family; inspired by the author's father's Supreme Court case about racially restrictive covenants in Chicago
back: A Raisin in the Sun (Lorraine Hansberry)

front: Play: Beneatha chooses between Nigerian suitor Joseph Asagai, who calls her hair straightening 'mutilation,' and the assimilated George Murchison
back: A Raisin in the Sun (Lorraine Hansberry)

front: Play: Karl Lindner, from the Clybourne Park Improvement Association, tries to bribe a family not to move into an all-white neighborhood
back: A Raisin in the Sun (Lorraine Hansberry)

front: Play: title from Langston Hughes's 'Harlem': 'What happens to a dream deferred?'; first play by a black woman on Broadway
back: A Raisin in the Sun (Lorraine Hansberry)

front: Play: Walter drunkenly shouts 'OCOMOGOSIAY!' and comes into his manhood 'like a rainbow after the rain' upon rejecting a buyout offer
back: A Raisin in the Sun (Lorraine Hansberry)

front: Play: a Jewish intellectual in Greenwich Village supports politician Wally O'Hara; Gloria commits suicide after Alton rejects her
back: The Sign in Sidney Brustein's Window (Lorraine Hansberry)
work: The Sign in Sidney Brustein's Window

front: Play: compiled from the author's writings by ex-husband Robert Nemiroff after her death; inspired a Nina Simone song
back: To Be Young, Gifted, and Black (Lorraine Hansberry)
work: To Be Young, Gifted, and Black

front: Play: left unfinished at author's death; Tshembe, a European intellectual, returns to Africa for his father's funeral; criticizes Genet's The Blacks
